title: atomic temp-file-then-rename in file backend is correct and worth defending
description: The write_secret_file() function uses create_new + write_all + rename, with best-effort temp file cleanup on error. This is the only correct pattern for atomic secret file writes — direct O_TRUNC write leaves an empty file on crash/OOM. The temp file uses .name.pid.counter.tmp in the same directory (same filesystem, so rename() is atomic). A contributor might suggest simplifying to fs::write() for clarity; that must be rejected because it is not atomic. The TEMP_COUNTER AtomicU64 handles concurrent put() calls within a single process.
